description: You are an archaeoastronomer studying the winter solstice sunrise alignment of Newgrange, Ireland's 5,200-year-old passage tomb. Reconstruct and compare the solstice sunrise at the time of construction (3200 BCE) versus today (2024 CE) using Stellarium. Configure the observer location for Newgrange (53.6947°N, 6.4755°W, altitude 75m) using the coordinates provided in /home/ga/data/archaeological_sites.json. For both December 21, -3199 and December 21, 2024 at 08:30 UTC, capture screenshots of the Sun centered on the horizon with the azimuthal grid and cardinal directions visible and the landscape disabled. Save screenshots to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/. Document your research notes in /home/ga/Desktop/newgrange_alignment_notes.txt, including the site name (Newgrange), solstice context, the ancient date, and the observed sunrise azimuth direction.

metadata.data_source: UNESCO World Heritage Centre, IAU Working Group on Archaeoastronomy
